name: Jawaharlal Nehru
key_policies: Non-Aligned Movement, Five Year Plans, IITs, Panchsheel
text: Jawaharlal Nehru: 1st Prime Minister of India (1947-1964). Indian National Congress. Served 17 years. Architect of modern India. Key policies: Non-Aligned Movement, Five Year Plans, establishment of IITs and scientific institutions, Panchsheel principles. Known as Pandit Nehru and Chacha Nehru.

name: Lal Bahadur Shastri
key_policies: Jai Jawan Jai Kisan, White Revolution, 1965 Indo-Pak War
text: Lal Bahadur Shastri: 2nd Prime Minister of India (1964-1966). INC. Famous slogan: Jai Jawan Jai Kisan. Led India during the 1965 Indo-Pakistan War. Promoted the White Revolution (Operation Flood). Died in Tashkent after signing the Tashkent Declaration.

name: Indira Gandhi
key_policies: Bank Nationalization, Green Revolution, Emergency, Garibi Hatao
text: Indira Gandhi: 3rd Prime Minister of India (1966-1977, 1980-1984). INC. Served 16 total years across two terms. Key: Bank nationalization (1969), Green Revolution, Garibi Hatao slogan, 1971 Bangladesh War, Emergency (1975-77). Assassinated October 31, 1984.

name: Morarji Desai
key_policies: First non-Congress PM, Janata Party coalition
text: Morarji Desai: 4th Prime Minister of India (1977-1979). Janata Party. First non-Congress PM. Former Finance Minister and Chief Minister of Bombay State. Received Bharat Ratna (1991) and Pakistan's Nishan-e-Pakistan.

name: Charan Singh
key_policies: Farmer advocacy, shortest-serving PM who never faced Parliament
text: Charan Singh: 5th Prime Minister of India (1979-1980). Janata Party (Secular). Served approximately 170 days. Never faced a vote of confidence in Parliament. Known as champion of farmers. Received posthumous Bharat Ratna in 2024.

name: Rajiv Gandhi
key_policies: Computer Revolution, Telecom expansion, Anti-Defection Law, Panchayati Raj
text: Rajiv Gandhi: 6th Prime Minister of India (1984-1989). INC. Youngest PM at age 40. Introduced computerization and telecom revolution. Anti-Defection Law (52nd Amendment). Panchayati Raj reforms. Assassinated May 21, 1991 during election campaign.

name: V.P. Singh
text: V.P. Singh: 7th Prime Minister of India (1989-1990). Janata Dal. Implemented the Mandal Commission recommendations for 27% OBC reservations in government jobs. This led to widespread protests and political upheaval.

name: Chandra Shekhar
key_policies: Gulf War crisis management, brief tenure with Congress support
text: Chandra Shekhar: 8th Prime Minister of India (1990-1991). Samajwadi Janata Party. Governed with outside support of INC. Managed the Gulf War crisis and evacuation of Indians from Kuwait. Served approximately 7 months.

name: P.V. Narasimha Rao
key_policies: 1991 Economic Liberalization, LPG reforms with Manmohan Singh
text: P.V. Narasimha Rao: 9th Prime Minister of India (1991-1996). INC. Architect of economic liberalization with Finance Minister Manmohan Singh. LPG (Liberalization, Privatization, Globalization) reforms. Opened India to foreign investment. Called Father of Indian Economic Reforms.

name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee
key_policies: Pokhran-II nuclear tests, Golden Quadrilateral, Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan
text: Atal Bihari Vajpayee: 10th Prime Minister of India (1996 for 13 days, then 1998-2004). BJP. Pokhran-II nuclear tests (1998). Golden Quadrilateral highway project. Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan. Kargil War (1999). Bharat Ratna 2015 (posthumous in effect). Revered orator and poet.

name: H.D. Deve Gowda
text: H.D. Deve Gowda: 11th Prime Minister of India (1996-1997). Janata Dal. Led the United Front coalition government. Former Chief Minister of Karnataka. Known for farmer-centric policies. Served approximately 11 months.

name: I.K. Gujral
key_policies: Gujral Doctrine on foreign policy with neighbors
text: I.K. Gujral: 12th Prime Minister of India (1997-1998). Janata Dal. Known for the Gujral Doctrine — India should give more to its smaller neighbors without expecting reciprocity. Former External Affairs Minister. Served approximately 11 months.

name: Manmohan Singh
key_policies: MGNREGA, RTI Act, Aadhaar, Indo-US Nuclear Deal, UPA coalition
text: Dr. Manmohan Singh: 13th Prime Minister of India (2004-2014). INC-led UPA. Served two terms. Architect of 1991 economic reforms as FM. Key achievements as PM: MGNREGA (2005), RTI Act (2005), Aadhaar system, Indo-US Civil Nuclear Agreement. Oxford and Cambridge educated economist.

name: Narendra Modi
key_policies: Digital India, Swachh Bharat, Make in India, GST, Demonetization, Ayushman Bharat, PM-KISAN
text: Narendra Modi: 14th and current Prime Minister of India since 2014, serving third consecutive term. BJP. Previously CM of Gujarat (2001-2014). Major reforms: GST implementation, demonetization (2016), Digital India, Swachh Bharat, Make in India, Ayushman Bharat, PM-KISAN, Atmanirbhar Bharat. Won 2014, 2019, 2024 Lok Sabha elections.
